records.ety: [L. hyaena, Gr. <?/, orig., a sow, but usually, a Libyan wild beast, prob., the hyena, fr. <?/ hog: cf. F. hyne. See Sow female hog.]

defs: Any carnivorous mammal of the family Hyænidæ, doglike nocturnal mammals of Africa and southern Asia, of which three living species are known. They are large and strong, but cowardly. They feed chiefly on carrion, and are nocturnal in their habits.
notes: ☞ The striped hyena (Hyæna striata) inhabits Southern Asia and a large part of Africa. The brown hyena (Hyæna brunnea), and the spotted hyena (Crocuta maculata), are found in Southern Africa. The extinct cave hyena (Hyæna spelæa) inhabited England and France.
